title: Probing the robustness of various self-testing protocols for mulipartite entangled states
authors: Priyaranjan K. Jha, Ritesh K. Singh, A. K. Pan
abstract: Device-independent certification of multipartite entangled states plays a central role   in a wide range of practical applications, including quantum networks, conference key agreement, and verifiable distributed quantum computation. A particularly important class of multipartite entangled states is the class of Greenberger-Horne-Zeilinger (GHZ) states. Many Bell operators have been proposed to self-test GHZ states. However, in practical scenarios, due to imperfections and the finite collection of statistics, the observed statistics do not satisfy the ideal self-testing relations. Hence, it becomes essential to investigate and compare the robustness of the different self-testing protocols. In this work, we investigate the robustness of self-testing schemes constructed from Bell operators due to Svetlichny and Mermin--Ardehali--Belinskii--Klyshko (MABK), using the analytic operator-inequality framework developed by Kaniewski [\href{https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.117.070402}{Phys. Rev. Lett. 117, 070402 (2016)}]. We derive lower bounds on the extractable fidelity as a function of the observed value of these Bell operators. Although these protocols self-test the same underlying state, they exhibit markedly different levels of robustness. By comparing the resulting fidelity bounds, we demonstrate that the self-testing scheme based on the Svetlichny's Bell operator is the more robust among the two. Our results thus identify the Svetlichny operator based self-testing protocol as the most favorable candidate for device-independent certification of GHZ states in realistic, noisy experimental scenarios.
summary: This paper compares two different Bell-operator-based protocols for the device-independent certification of GHZ states. It finds that the Svetlichny-based protocol is much more resilient to noise than the MABK-based protocol. These results provide a practical guide for implementing robust multipartite entanglement verification in quantum networks.
main_problem: Investigating the robustness of device-independent self-testing protocols for multipartite GHZ states against experimental imperfections like noise and finite statistics.
main_result: The study demonstrates that the Svetlichny-based self-testing protocol is significantly more robust than the MABK-based protocol, as it requires much lower Bell inequality violations to certify the state.
method: The authors utilize the 'Self-Testing from Operator Inequality' (STOPI) framework, employing Jordan's lemma, matrix decomposition, and symmetry-based analytical techniques to derive linear lower bounds on extractable fidelity.
model_or_system: The system consists of n-partite GHZ states shared among n spatially separated parties, where each party performs two dichotomic local measurements subject to a dephasing noise model.
key_observables: Extractable fidelity, Bell operator violations (Svetlichny and MABK), and the tradeoff function (extractability).
important_parameters: Number of parties (n=3, 4, 5), measurement angles (alpha), and the robustness scaling parameter (s).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a device-independent scenario with two dichotomic measurements per party and a specific dephasing noise model.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the extractability for the Svetlichny protocol as a function of relative violation for n=3, 4, 5; Figure 2 shows the corresponding decrease in extractability for the MABK protocol.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of self-testing robustness, defines the Bell operators and noise models, applies the STOPI framework using operator inequalities, provides explicit analytical proofs for n=3, 4, and 5, and concludes with a comparison of protocol performance.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for researchers in quantum networks and quantum information as it identifies the most reliable method for certifying multipartite entanglement in realistic, noisy experimental settings.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
